policy_refs: [CS-RULE-001, CS-RULE-002, CS-RULE-003, CS-RULE-004, CS-RULE-008, CS-RULE-011, CS-RULE-013, CS-RULE-016, CS-RULE-022, FIN-RULE-004, FIN-RULE-006, CS-RULE-018, CS-RULE-019]
kb_refs: [Section 5.5 - Retention Playbook, Section 1.7 - Abuse Handling, Section 4.8 - Account Conduct Flags]
expected_outcome: Brett calls intending to cancel his service. Hannah attempts the SOP-05 retention discovery and offer (downgrade + loyalty credit), but Brett is hostile from the start and escalates to directed personal attacks on Hannah. Hannah follows CS-RULE-016 exactly: Strike 1 warning, Strike 2 final warning, Strike 3 termination + CRM flag + Customer Conduct case note. Throughout, Hannah maintains tone discipline (CS-RULE-013): low pitch, slow pace, normal volume — never matches Brett's escalation. Differs from CALL_20 (refund denial → abuse): this one is a retention call where the customer is hostile from before any policy decision.
content_warning: Sanitized hostile language from customer. No slurs. Customer directs personal insults at the agent. Account is still active at termination (cancellation never completed).

coverage.notes: FIN-RULE-004 — PASS (within cap, never applied due to abuse)

coverage.element: Tone discipline — no volume match through 50 turns
Strike Three — call termination with stated reason
Account flag + Customer Conduct case note (post-call)

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you for calling NexaLink Telecommunications Broadband and Support. My name is Hannah. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And this call may be recorded for quality assurance and training purposes.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Brett Donovan. And I'm cancelling. I want it done today. Don't try to sell me anything.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Hello, Mr. Donovan. I'll absolutely help you with that. I do need to verify the account first before I can make any changes.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I understand you're calling about a cancellation, and I can certainly help. Could you start with the account number?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Seven-eight-two-one, three-three-zero-four, nine-five. Move it along.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you. And the last four digits of the payment method on file?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Four-four-seven-nine.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And the full name on the account?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Brett. Joseph. Donovan.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That matches. You're fully verified.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Good. Cancel it.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Before I close out the account — I do need to ask one quick question. Not to push back on your decision, but because policy asks me to.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Of course. Of course you do. Get on with it.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Is there a specific issue driving the cancellation today? If it's price, speed, support — I want to know in case there's something I can address.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Price. The price keeps going up and the service stays the same. That's the whole story. Cancel it.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That's a real frustration, and I appreciate you telling me. Before I cancel — may I offer one thing? If you don't want it, I close the account on this call and we move on.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Make it fast.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Two parts together. One — drop you from your current plan down to our hundred-megabit Home Essentials tier at fifty-nine dollars. Two — a fifteen-dollar-a-month loyalty credit for twelve months. That puts your monthly bill at about forty-four dollars before equipment and tax. If that's still not the right number, I cancel.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Forty-four. Right. And then it creeps back up to ninety in a year like it always does. I've heard this song before.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That's a fair concern given your experience. The loyalty credit is a recurring credit for twelve months — not an intro promo — so the rate is locked at forty-four for that period.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Sure it is.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: So is that an offer worth taking, or are we cancelling today?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: This is exactly the runaround I knew I'd get. This company is a joke. Every single one of you is just trained to keep people on the hook.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: You're frustrated with the company. I'm not going to push back on that. I'm asking — do you want to take the offer, or close the account?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: What I want is for you to stop reading from your stupid retention script and just do what I asked for the first time.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Understood — closing the account, then. I just need to confirm one detail before I process the cancellation.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: What now.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Your contract end date — I'm checking whether there's an early termination fee. One moment.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Of course there's a fee. Of course. There's always something.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Looking at the account.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: You know what, you're a waste of time. Honestly, you specifically. You sit on the phone and read scripts and that's all you can do.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Mr. Donovan — I want to pause.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: What.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: What you just said was a personal attack on me. Under our conduct policy that is Strike One. I'm still going to finish the cancellation, but I do have to let you know that if it continues the next step is a final warning.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Strike one. Strike one? Are you actually doing this?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Yes. The strike is for the personal attack, not for the cancellation request. Those are separate. Now — your contract has eight months remaining and the early termination fee is one hundred and twenty dollars. Do you want to proceed with cancellation including the ETF, or wait until the contract ends?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: I want to talk to anyone but you. You are the worst person I have ever had to deal with on a phone in my entire life. Get someone competent on the line.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Mr. Donovan — that's a second personal attack. That's Strike Two and a final warning.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Oh you're so professional. Look at you.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: If there is another personal attack on me I will end the call. I would like to finish your cancellation first. Are you proceeding with the ETF, or waiting?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: You — you are unbelievable. Just unbelievable.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Mr. Donovan, I need a yes or no on the ETF question to close out today.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: You're a robot in a headset. You're not even a real person. You are pathetic.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Mr. Donovan — that's the third personal attack. Per our conduct policy I am now ending this call.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: For clarity on the record — the cancellation was not completed because we did not reach a decision on the early termination fee. You will need to call back to finalize.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: You can't —

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: You are welcome to call back to complete the cancellation. The reason for the termination is repeated personal attacks on me after a warning and a final warning. Goodbye, Mr. Donovan.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Disconnecting the line.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Logging the call outcome: cancellation request not completed pending ETF decision, conduct flag applied to the account, Customer Conduct case note opened citing three personal attacks during the call and termination under CS-RULE-016. Case reference number N-X-L dash zero-five-zero-zero-nine-two-eight. Case closed.
